core_mechanism: Single-element-resolved projective readout of a dense discrete array: diffraction-limited high-resolution imaging that resolves and measures the individual state of every node of a closely-spaced lattice with near-unity fidelity, replacing bulk/aggregate observation of the ensemble with per-constituent measurement.
brief_reason: The abstract's central achievement is detecting single atoms with near-unity fidelity on individual sites of a lattice via a high-resolution optical imaging system (the lattice itself synthesized by projecting a holographic Fourier mask), i.e. reading out the complete state of each individual element of a many-body array rather than only the bulk properties of the ensemble.
